scenes.title: Scene 1
scenes.name: Survey Village
teach: Data is information collected to answer a question.
We collect data to understand people, events, or results.
A good data collection question is clear and fair.
Biased questions can make data unreliable.
worked: Question: What is your favourite fruit?
Why is a biased question bad?
It pushes people toward one answer.

scenes.title: Scene 2
scenes.name: Data Laboratory
teach: Categorical data uses names or groups.
Numerical data uses numbers.
Discrete data is counted.
Continuous data is measured.

scenes.title: Scene 3
scenes.name: Questionnaire City
teach: A questionnaire is a list of questions.
Closed questions have fixed choices.
Open questions allow longer answers.
Questions should be simple, clear, and neutral.

scenes.title: Scene 4
scenes.name: Tally Forest
teach: A tally chart records data quickly.
Four tally marks are written normally.
The fifth tally mark crosses the first four.
Groups of five are easy to count.

scenes.title: Scene 5
scenes.name: Frequency Space
teach: Frequency means how often something happens.
A frequency table organises categories and counts.
Total frequency means all frequencies added together.

scenes.title: Scene 6
scenes.name: Sampling Jungle
teach: A population is the whole group being studied.
A sample is a smaller group from the population.
A good sample should represent the population.
A biased sample gives unfair results.
worked: Only football players surveyed about sport.

scenes.title: Scene 7
scenes.name: Data Castle
teach: Raw data is data before it is organised.
Tables help organise data clearly.
Organised data makes patterns easier to see.

scenes.title: Scene 8
scenes.name: Dragon Fortress
teach: Read the question carefully.
For greater questions, compare numeric values, not text.
Commas and trailing zeros are normalised before checking.
